mission.title: apisix: help request:  Alternatives to Semantic Pull Requests
mission.description: help request:  Alternatives to Semantic Pull Requests. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current apisix deployment
steps.description:
Verify your apisix version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
helm list -n apisix 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working apisix installation.

steps.title: Review apisix configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant apisix configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get configmap -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=apisix
```
Currently we are using [Semantic Pull Requests](https://github.com/

steps.title: Apply the fix for help request:  Alternatives to Semantic Pull Requests
steps.description:
Note it:

```
We only allow Actions that are official "Made by GitHub" or local to the Apache org on GitHub, to address a potential security vulnerability. This is an incident-related policy change. We are researching the situation, and the policy may evolve based on what we learn.
```

https://infra.apache.org/github-actions-secrets.html

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n apisix -l app.kubernetes.io/name=apisix
kubectl get events -n apisix --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "help request:  Alternatives to Semantic Pull Requests" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Note it:

```
We only allow Actions that are official "Made by GitHub" or local to the Apache org on GitHub, to address a potential security vulnerability. This is an incident-related policy change. We are researching the situation, and the policy may evolve based on what we learn.
```

https://infra.apache.org/github-actions-secrets.html

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with apisix installed or the issue environment reproducible.
